notes: "These (ID-01001 to 01008) are Micrasema (gelidum) gelidum R McLachlan, 1876. Three females (54-56) are a different haplotype from the males (58-59) and the other females (52-53, 57). All females were collected at the same locality on the same day, both males were collected the previous day 4 km downstream of the females. Females are the only gender falling into the 2 haplotypes, but females of Micrasema are indistinguishable morphologically, especially females of the same "prospecies". To determine whether these two haplotypes are cryptic species, it will be necessary to compare many males of the 2 haplotypes in order to discover any consistent, albeit slight, morphological differences."-John Morse, Apr 19, 2010.

notes: "These (ID-01001 to 01008) are Micrasema (gelidum) gelidum R McLachlan, 1876. Three females (54-56) are a different haplotype from the males (58-59) and the other females (52-53, 57). All females were collected at the same locality on the same day, both males were collected the previous day 4 km downstream of the females. Females are the only gender falling into the 2 haplotypes, but females of Micrasema are indistinguishable morphologically, especially females of the same "prospecies". To determine whether these two haplotypes are cryptic species, it will be necessary to compare many males of the 2 haplotypes in order to discover any consistent, albeit slight, morphological differences."-John Morse, Apr 19, 2010.

notes: "These (ID-01001 to 01008) are Micrasema (gelidum) gelidum R McLachlan, 1876. Three females (54-56) are a different haplotype from the males (58-59) and the other females (52-53, 57). All females were collected at the same locality on the same day, both males were collected the previous day 4 km downstream of the females. Females are the only gender falling into the 2 haplotypes, but females of Micrasema are indistinguishable morphologically, especially females of the same "prospecies". To determine whether these two haplotypes are cryptic species, it will be necessary to compare many males of the 2 haplotypes in order to discover any consistent, albeit slight, morphological differences."-John Morse, Apr 19, 2010.

notes: "These (ID-01001 to 01008) are Micrasema (gelidum) gelidum R McLachlan, 1876. Three females (54-56) are a different haplotype from the males (58-59) and the other females (52-53, 57). All females were collected at the same locality on the same day, both males were collected the previous day 4 km downstream of the females. Females are the only gender falling into the 2 haplotypes, but females of Micrasema are indistinguishable morphologically, especially females of the same "prospecies". To determine whether these two haplotypes are cryptic species, it will be necessary to compare many males of the 2 haplotypes in order to discover any consistent, albeit slight, morphological differences."-John Morse, Apr 19, 2010.

notes: "These (ID-01001 to 01008) are Micrasema (gelidum) gelidum R McLachlan, 1876. Three females (54-56) are a different haplotype from the males (58-59) and the other females (52-53, 57). All females were collected at the same locality on the same day, both males were collected the previous day 4 km downstream of the females. Females are the only gender falling into the 2 haplotypes, but females of Micrasema are indistinguishable morphologically, especially females of the same "prospecies". To determine whether these two haplotypes are cryptic species, it will be necessary to compare many males of the 2 haplotypes in order to discover any consistent, albeit slight, morphological differences."-John Morse, Apr 19, 2010.
